channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: From the Concept to Reality: Building "Ez Garden Visualizer" in Stages
item.description: So Many Possibilities... So Little Time When I first thought about building an AI garden visualizer app, the full idea sounded much bigger than a weekend project: upload a garden photo, generate a...

item.title: What Happens During an Incident (Part 4)
item.description: In Part 3, we separated signals on purpose: metrics tell you where to look logs and traces tell you what happened audit tells you what can be proven later. This article is an incident story. The...

item.title: 6 architectures I considered for a privacy-first personal SaaS — and why I built two of them
item.description: Every architectural decision I made building OvertimeIQ came from the same four constraints. Not preferences — constraints. Things that were non-negotiable before I wrote a line of code. Work data...

item.title: I asked Cursor to rename a function. It sent 8,400 tokens. I checked.
item.description: The afternoon I learned what my AI subscription was actually doing, and the 200 lines that took my next bill down 41 percent. I had been using Cursor for six months when I noticed the discrepancy....

item.title: I built an AI Agent that lives directly in your CLI and Desktop
item.description: Let's be real: the current AI coding workflow is tedious. You hit a bug. You copy your code. You tab out to ChatGPT/Claude. You paste it. You copy the fix. You paste it back. Oh wait, it broke...

item.title: I Stopped Using Claude Code as a Giant Prompt and Started Using It as Project Ops
item.description: If you use Claude Code on a real project for more than one-off coding tasks, you eventually hit the same wall: the model is good at solving the task in front of it, but every new session still has...

item.title: Super App Architecture: Technical Design Patterns for Building Scalable Ecosystem Platforms
item.description: 1. What is a Super App? A Super App is not just an app with many features. It is a platform that hosts other apps, creating an ecosystem where third-party developers can build and distribute...

item.title: FuriosaAI vs. Nvidia: Who Leads AI Inference Efficiency?
item.description: We're living in an exciting era for AI, where the cutting edge isn't just about bigger models, but smarter, smaller ones. Projects like Needle's distilled Gemini, aiming to pack powerful AI into...

item.title: 💻 How to vm migrate from vmware to kvm — key tips and pitfalls
item.description: Two virtual machines, identical in configuration and OS, migrated from VMware to KVM using different tools: one completes in 22 minutes with full network functionality; the other fails after 45...

item.title: I Turned Photos Into ASCII Art Without a Single Server Call—Here's How
item.description: Remember when ASCII art was just something you pasted into IRC channels? I always thought it was a neat party trick until I tried building an image-to-ASCII converter that runs entirely in the...

item.title: Sony’s new Xperia phone gets an overdue redesign
item.description: Sony's Xperia 1 flagships have looked more or less the same since 2020, but that's finally changing with the Xperia 1 VIII, which moves to a chunky square camera island. The phone also boasts what...

item.title: Medicare’s new payment model is built for AI, and most of the tech world has no idea
item.description: There is no governmental mechanism to pay for an AI agent that monitors a patient between visits, calls to check in, coordinates a housing referral, or makes sure someone picks up their...

item.title: Elon Musk Had ‘Hair-Raising’ Idea of Passing OpenAI On to His Kids, Sam Altman Says
item.description: Musk’s lawyers questioned Altman over allegations of deception and his network of financial investments, but the OpenAI CEO painted a picture of Musk as obsessed with controlling the company.

item.title: xAI Adds 19 New Gas Turbines Despite Ongoing Lawsuit
item.description: Emails show that Elon Musk’s company is expanding its use of portable gas-fired power at its Colossus 2 site as a fight over air quality continues.

item.title: Sam Altman was winning on the stand, but it might not be enough
item.description: After two weeks of hearing from assorted witnesses that he was a lying snake, the jury finally heard from the lying snake himself: Sam Altman. At the end of the testimony, his lawyer William...

item.title: The Unitree GD01 Is a Giant Mecha Robot You Can Actually Buy
item.description: China’s Unitree, famous for making low-cost dancing robots, will now sell you a giant, wall-smashing mecha.

item.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
item.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer...

item.title: iOS 27 might add a lot more customization to the Camera app
item.description: Apple's next iOS update could include something phone photographers have been waiting for: a lot more control over the Camera app. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, the Camera app will be...

item.title: Meta won’t let you block its AI account on Threads
item.description: Meta announced on Tuesday that it's testing a Threads feature that lets users tag a Meta AI account to get answers to questions or context about a conversation on the platform. If you've spent any...

item.title: Meta will tell parents when their teens add new interests to their Instagram algorithm
item.description: Starting Tuesday, parents can get more details about what their teens are up to on Instagram. Meta is adding a feature to its Teen Accounts that shows parents the "general topics their teens...

item.title: Musk mulled handing OpenAI to his children, Altman testifies
item.description: Altman said that Musk's focus on controlling the initial for-profit gave him pause because OpenAI was dedicated to keeping advanced AI out of the hands of a single person, and Altman, with his...

item.title: Anthropic warns investors against secondary platforms offering access to its shares
item.description: "Any sale or transfer of Anthropic stock, or any interest in Anthropic stock, offered by these firms is void and will not be recognized on our books and records," the company's support page reads.

item.title: Sam Altman says Elon Musk’s mind games were damaging OpenAI
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman says Elon Musk did "huge damage" to the culture of the AI startup. During testimony as part of Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, Altman said Musk required OpenAI president Greg...

item.title: Report: Google and SpaceX in talks to put data centers into orbit
item.description: Google and SpaceX are in talks to build data centers in orbit, pitching space as the future home for AI compute, even as costs today remain far higher than on the ground.

item.title: Everything Google announced at its Android Show, from Googlebooks to vibe-coded widgets
item.description: Google unveiled its new AI-first Googlebooks laptops, more agentic Gemini features, vibe-coded Android widgets, Gemini in Chrome, refreshed Android Auto, and more ahead of I/O.

item.title: Google adds Gemini-powered dictation to Gboard, which could be bad news for dictation startups
item.description: Google's transcription feature will initially launch with Samsung Galaxy and Google Pixel phones.

item.title: The AI legal services industry is heating up — Anthropic is getting in on the action
item.description: Anthropic's new tools are designed to help law firms automate specific clerical functions — things like document search and review, case law resources, deposition prep, document drafting, and...

item.title: Android Auto is now one (screen) size fits all
item.description: This week at I/O, Google rolled out a series of major updates for Android Auto that include better sizing for unconventionally shaped screens, video streaming through YouTube, widget support, and...

item.title: Google announces its Chromebook successor: the Googlebook
item.description: Google is announcing a new line of laptops coming in the fall called Googlebooks. Details are sparse for now, as the tease is just a small part of various Android announcements during Google's...

item.title: The 9 biggest new features in Android 17
item.description: Would it shock you to hear that Android 17 is filled with new AI-enabled features, like improved dictation and vibe-coded widgets? Fortunately, that's not all. The platform is getting non-AI...
